channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What to do if your manager keeps watching your Instagram Stories
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Leaked Galaxy S25 benchmarks apparently confirm an all-Snapdragon line-up for 2025
item.description: New benchmarks suggest all of the Galaxy S25 models across the globe are getting Snapdragon chipsets.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple could launch the iPhone SE 4 as the iPhone 16E, according to rumors
item.description: The handset previously known as the iPhone SE 4 could in fact become the iPhone 16E, according to leaks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Intro to VSCode.dev: The IDE in your browser
item.description: The fully realized browser-based IDE has been a long time coming. Ever since the beginning of the cloud revolution, I’ve thought a powerful and flexible remote IDE was just around the corner. I imagined a development tool that could use any virtual machine as a development platform.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rhode Island’s state health benefits website hacked, user data exposed
item.description: Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: LG will debut its latest smart appliances at CES, and I'm ready to throw down my life savings for its fully transparent disco-ready T-OLED refrigerator display
item.description: LG has announced its second-generation smart appliances, including a refrigerator with a fully-transparent screen.
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: AI vaporware: 7 products that didn’t materialize in 2024
item.description: Whether to raise money, placate shareholders, or generate positive press, AI’s biggest companies have a habit of announcing advancements that are nowhere near ready to ship.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The long-term impacts of AI on networking
item.description: AI is either going to spawn a golden age of profits and benefits or kill us all off, according to popular viewpoints on the subject. The latter view has been at least somewhat discredited; if you yell “AI” in a crowded theater these days, hardly anyone runs for the exits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Leaked codenames point to the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7, Z Flip 7, and Z Fold SE 2
item.description: It looks as though there are at least three foldable phones on the way from Samsung through the course of 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Hands-on with Adobe’s Acrobat AI Assistant: AI for your PDFs
item.description: We may be on the brink of 2025, but PDFs are still unavoidable in the professional world. No matter what industry you work in, you’re bound to whittle away precious moments wading through reports, white papers, and other dense documents in that clunky-feeling form.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meet the mad scientist of headphones, whose wild designs electrify Reddit
item.description: The standard shape for big, chunky headphones has long been a rainbow with Moon Pies at either end. No rule exists, however, forcing audio companies to keep making them that way. Philip Kaplan, the tech entrepreneur behind Tiny Letter and DistroKid, has been proving as much all year.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to delay TikTok ban so he can weigh in after he takes office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has asked the Supreme Court to pause the potential TikTok ban from going into effect until his administration can pursue a “political resolution” to the issue.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New York state government to monitor its use of AI under a new law
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York state government agencies will have to conduct reviews and publish reports that detail how they're using artificial intelligence software, under a new law signed by Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Critical warning from Microsoft: .NET install domains changing
item.description: Users who expected to be able to use the resources of the Azure content delivery network (CDN) from Edgio until November 2025, as previously announced, had a not-so-festive “gift” this week – the CDN is now shutting down on Jan. 15, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: An online debate over foreign workers in tech shows tensions in Trump's political coalition
item.description: An online spat between factions of Donald Trump’s supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in the president-elect’s political movement into public display.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Undersea power cable linking Finland and Estonia hit by outage, prompting investigation
item.description: FRANKFURT, Germany (AP) — A power cable linking Finland and Estonia under the Baltic Sea suffered an outage, prompting an investigation, Finnish Prime Minister Petteri Orpo said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
